content_preview.title: Bungie Ends Active Development of Destiny 2 After Nine Years, Final Content Update Ships June 9
content_preview.summary: Bungie announced on May 21 that Monument of Triumph, launching June 9, will be Destiny 2's last live-service update, closing a nine-year live-service run as Sony absorbs a $765 million write-down on the studio.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Bungie announced on May 21 that it is ending active development on *Destiny 2*, its flagship live-service shooter that has run continuously since 2017. The final content update, *Monument of Triumph*, will launch on June 9, 2026, as a free release for all players. After that date, the game will enter a maintenance-only state — no new seasonal content, no expansions — though Bungie has said it will remain playable indefinitely, following the same model as the original *Destiny*, [acc...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured news article covering a significant gaming industry event. Clear sections, good use of subheadings, and a dedicated 'What We Don't Know' section that keeps claims appropriately bounded. The article is factually dense but well-organized for a 1,106-word news piece.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 8 sources fetched successfully. Sources read from disk: source-0 (gamedeveloper.com), source-1 (outof.games), source-2 (kotaku.com), source-3 (gamingbible.com), source-4 (techtimes.com/316991), source-5 (pushsquare.com), source-6 (techtimes.com/317034), source-7 (thesixthaxis.com). Most claims verified. Key findings: (1) The article states Marathon 'earned more than 90 percent positive Steam reviews' and attributes this to Push Square (source-5). Push Square does not mention a percentage. TechTimes (source-6) states the actual figure was '84.8% rating on Steam at launch' — a 'Very Positive' Steam label, not 'more than 90 percent.' This is a fabricated specific in the article body. (2) The Bungie quote attributed to Gaming Bible: the article renders 'twelve years' where Gaming Bible's source-3 snapshot reads '12 years' — minor numeral vs spelled-out discrepancy, not substantive. The spelling 'honour' (British) matches Gaming Bible. (3) All other key specifics verified: June 9 final update date, Monument of Triumph features (Director return, Pantheon 2.0, Sparrow Racing League, Legendary Marks, Crucible updates, Gambit promotion, Exotic transmog, seasonal events retired), layoff numbers (Oct 2023: ~100/8%, July 2024: 220/17% + 155 moved to SIE), Sony $765M write-down with $204M component from Push Square, Pete Parsons stepping down August 2025 and Justin Truman as replacement (TechTimes source-6). The outof.games site is not on the allowlist but is an established gaming outlet. Domains gamingbible.com, techtimes.com, pushsquare.com, and thesixthaxis.com are similarly unlisted but credible gaming and tech outlets.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The headline, summary, and lead are all accurate and supported by sources. One fabricated specific in the body: 'more than 90 percent positive Steam reviews' for Marathon — actual figure was 84.8% at launch per TechTimes. This is not in the headline or summary and can be addressed via a corrections record.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The 'Troubled Road to This Moment' section covers the studio's difficulties in factual terms without editorializing. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Bungie's Destiny 2 shutdown. The article correctly cross-references the prior Marathon Server Slam piece from 2026-03-02 with a relative link. Not a duplicate.
concerns: The 'more than 90 percent positive Steam reviews' claim for Marathon is not supported by the cited Push Square source and conflicts with TechTimes reporting (84.8% at launch). This requires a corrections record.
recommendations: Add pushsquare.com, techtimes.com, gamingbible.com, outof.games, and thesixthaxis.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all are established gaming outlets regularly used in gaming coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong news article covering an important gaming industry event. All headline, summary, and lead claims are verified. One minor fabricated specific in the body (Marathon Steam review percentage) can be addressed via a corrections record. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
